parent.text: The world’s first sub‑1 nanometer node chip is here. Delivering 70% greater energy efficiency, this breakthrough powers a new era of computing that’s more capable while using less energy. Dig into this next-gen tech:
parent.name: IBM News
parent.embed_html:
The world’s first sub‑1 nanometer node chip is here.

Delivering 70% greater energy efficiency, this breakthrough powers a new era of computing that’s more capable while using less energy.

Dig into this next-gen tech: https://t.co/NkzAahH49S pic.twitter.com/zfgZK77iu4

— IBM News (@IBMNews) June 25, 2026

ai_summary: IBM announced a breakthrough sub-1 nanometer chip node with 70% greater energy efficiency, and Musk questioned the actual atomic-scale dimensions of the smallest feature size—a critical technical clarification. This reflects the engineering rigor required when evaluating semiconductor advances, as marketing claims about 'node names' often don't correspond to literal feature sizes, a key consideration for companies like Tesla and SpaceX that depend on cutting-edge chip performance for autonomous systems and avionics.
embed_html:
How many atoms wide is the smallest feature size?

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 26, 2026
